When is my bin collected?

Details of your bin collection days for the waste and recycling service in Torbay.

Your recycling boxes, blue bag for paper, food bin and wheeled bin or seagull proof bag need to be placed at the boundary of your property so easily accessible from the adopted public highway by 6am on your collection day. We may need to vary the times of collection so please make sure that you don’t put it out late. Recycling and waste containers must not be left or stored on the highway or footpath between scheduled collections. Only bins and containers provided by Torbay Council/SWISCo will be emptied, to ensure that they meet the required standards for safe and efficient collection.

Find out when your bins are collected

  Find your bin collection dates Opens in a new window

Recycling

Each property is supplied with two recycling boxes which are emptied weekly. Please make sure that your recycling is separated into the correct box. You can request new stickers to show you what should go into each of the boxes. Please also ensure you are using recycling box covers/nets to prevent waste from being blown out onto the street. 

Request new bin stickers

Households are provided with both a small food caddy to use in the kitchen and a larger food waste bin to store outside. You can use the kitchen caddy to decant food waste to your kerbside caddy. The kitchen food caddy can be lined with any type of plastic bag; newspaper or special caddy liners to keep it clean. The large food waste bin will be emptied weekly.

Residual waste

Most households are provided with a wheeled bin for any general household rubbish that cannot be recycled. This will be collected fortnightly.

Properties that aren’t suitable for wheeled bins are provided with seagull proof sacks and in almost all cases will receive a weekly collection.

If you live in a block of flats please see our page about flats and communal collections.

Properties with restricted access

  • Where access is restricted due to the width or design of the highway, alternative or specially adapted vehicles may be used to collect recycling, food waste, garden waste and residual waste.
  • Where access is controlled by electronic gates or other security barriers, residents or their agent must accommodate the arrival of the collection teams and provide timely entry.
  • Where access is not permitted to gated properties or properties that can only be accesses from the unadopted highway, residents must place their bins and containers outside the gates or at the edge of the public highway, for collection.
  • Where development of new properties is still taking place and roads not yet adopted, but residents are in occupation, SWISCo will carry out a risk assessment to determine whether it is safe to enter the site to make collections.
This page was last updated over three months ago

xhrs.body:
{"banner":{"title":"Privacy and cookies","description":"

We use cookies to collect information about how you use our website. We use this information to make the website work as well as possible and see how we can improve our services.

"},"success":{"title":"Settings saved","description":"

Your cookie preferences have been saved on this device for the next 90 days.

If you wish to change these at any time see our cookies page.

"},"introduction":"

We use cookies to make our website work and to ensure you get the best experience when you use it. We'd also like to set optional analytics cookies to help us improve it and to monitor use. We won't set optional cookies unless you enable them. Using this tool will set a cookie on your device to remember your preferences. Learn more about the cookies we use.

","cookies":{"essential":[{"id":"essential","name":"Essential cookies","description":"

We need these to make the website work, so these will always be there, but don't worry they are good cookies and don't collect any information about you.

"}],"manageable":[{"id":"ga","name":"Google Analytics","description":"

These cookies allow us to count the number of visitors using the website and see how they use it. They don't collect any information that identifies you.

We only use this to improve how our website works. Google Universal Analytics has been superseded by Google Tag Manager, avoid all Google tracking disable both Google Analytics and Google Tag Manager options.

​

"},{"id":"fbp","name":"Facebook Pixel","description":"

Facebook Pixel is an analytics tool that allows us to measure the effectiveness of our posts and advertising on Facebook by understanding the actions that you take on our website.

This can also be used to target Facebook adverts based on the pages you view.

"},{"id":"gtm","name":"Google Tag Manager","description":"

These cookies allow us to manage and track specific tasks or events carried out on our website.

Google Tag Manager also loads Google Analytics GA4, ReciteMe assistive toolbar and Clarity Analytics. These cookies help us understand how people use our website. They show us:

  • what people click on
  • how far they scroll
  • where they move their mouse
  • which pages they visit
  • the number of visitors to the website

They also tell us what kind of device is used, where the person is, and what language they prefer.

These cookies do not collect your name or any personal details. They are not used on parts of the site where you need to log in. We only use this information to make the website better.

"},{"id":"ssi","name":"Orlo","description":"

We use Orlo to manage our social media accounts. This cookie helps us to track how our posts perform and link back to our website.

"},{"id":"can","name":"Council Advertising Network","description":"

These cookies are used by partners in the Council Advertising Network to collect your personal or non-personal data which is then used to provide you with interest-based and behavioural advertising.

"}]}}
